iti47:1.1: This was said by the Blessed One, said by the Arahant, so I have heard:
iti47:2.1:
“Monks, a monk should be wakeful:
mindful, alert, centered, sensitive, clear, & calm.
And there he should, at the appropriate times, see clearly into skillful mental qualities.
iti47:2.2:
For a monk who is wakeful–mindful, alert, centered, sensitive, clear, & calm, seeing clearly,
at the appropriate times, into skillful mental qualities–one of two fruits can be expected:
iti47:2.3: either gnosis right in the here-&-now, or–if there be any remnant of clinging-sustenance–non-return.”
iti47:3.1: Those who are wakeful, listen to this!
iti47:3.2: Those who are sleeping, wake up!
iti47:3.4: For those who are wakeful, there’s no danger, no fear.
iti47:4.3: rightly exploring the Dhamma at appropriate times,
iti47:4.4: he–on becoming unified– could shatter the darkness.
iti47:5.3: cutting the fetter of birth & aging–
iti47:5.4: touches right here a self-awakening un- surpassed.
